assumption: Use low-field Hall measurements at <=300 G and very low electric field to determine carrier density at 4.2 K
Treat log transverse resistivity versus log magnetic field as approximately linear over selected field intervals
notes: Authors explicitly state that no exact power law was found in most samples

assumption: Use a Thomas-Fermi estimate of impurity-potential range and compare Fermi wavelength, impurity range, and magnetic length
notes: Most samples satisfy lambda_F > r_s > R

assumption: Interpret the observed exponent using self-consistent transport calculations for long- and short-range impurity scattering
notes: Comparison is qualitative rather than a parameter inversion

assumption: Treat Kane-model nonparabolicity as an approximate additional B^0.5 contribution to magnetoresistance
notes: No quantitative low-temperature HgCdTe calculation was available

assumption: Use only high-mobility samples with marked Shubnikov-de Haas oscillations and compare disk and bar geometries
notes: Finite contact-size error estimated below 5 percent

assumption: Assume surface contributions are not dominant after etching and repeat checks while acknowledging they cannot be fully excluded
notes: No source covariance or quantitative surface correction is reported
